what_it_does: Loads and validates a JSON registry of render declarations, maps raw session events to typed render declarations through a cascading key lookup, extracts fields via dotted paths with visible truncation, and emits drop records for undeclared or family-fallback events.
observations: The registry is loaded from an adjacent render_declarations.json file and cached under a threading.Lock.
Validation enforces closed vocabularies for surfaces, placements, components, evidence, update_targets, and stream_accumulators, plus required keys and non-empty dotted field_map paths.
Lookup order is exact type/subtype, family type/*, bare type, then the loud UNDECLARED UnknownEvent path.
Special dispatch handles stream_event inner types, content_block_start/delta block types, attachment types, and assistant/user content blocks including tool-name refinement.
Unknown events still render as UnknownEvent with undeclared:true and fire drop hooks; family wildcard hits render but set family_fallback:true and also drop.
Field extraction omits missing paths and caps long strings at FIELD_CAP with a __truncated marker.
Drop hooks let external consumers observe registry gaps; hook exceptions are swallowed so declaration logic is not interrupted.
standouts: Declaration-driven rendering: the UI renders from a validated registry rather than per-event bespoke logic.
Loud failure design: RegistryInvalid at load, UnknownEvent for undeclared events, visible truncation markers, and explicit family_fallback drops.
Closed-vocabulary validation with fail-loud schema enforcement.
conventions: Follows module-level constants, a cached global registry with a lock, type annotations, and docstring-heavy style.
Uses underscore-prefixed names for internal helpers.
Breaks typical purity by maintaining global mutable state for the registry and drop hooks, and by silently swallowing hook exceptions.
concerns: _fire_drop catches all Exception from hooks, which can mask hook bugs.
_lookup detects family fallback by key.endswith('*'), which is brittle if an exact key ever ends with *.
derive_key uses '?' placeholders for missing type fields, producing candidates that will not match anything before falling back to undeclared.
If blocks_from resolves to a non-list value, the content blocks are silently omitted.
Global mutable _REGISTRY and _DROP_HOOKS make concurrency and test isolation harder to reason about.
FIELD_CAP measures Python string length, not bytes or grapheme clusters, which may not match intended truncation semantics.
notes: A registry-backed event-to-render-declaration mapper that makes rendering explicit, validated, and loud about registry gaps.
questions: What is the exact shape and vocabulary of render_declarations.json?
How are drop hooks registered in production, and is their ordering significant?
What are the full closed vocabularies for surfaces, placements, components, etc.?
How does ops/render_declared_stream.py consume these declarations?
Are the R1.2/R11/heart-frame terms defined in other files?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it loads and validates a registry, maps events to declarations, handles unknowns loudly, and extracts fields honestly.
apparent_themes: Declarative rendering over imperative event handling
intention_signals: Decouple event emission from UI rendering by introducing a typed, validated intermediary declaration layer, and make registry incompleteness observable rather than hidden.
novelty: The idea that every session output declares its own render shape via a validated registry, using cascading specificity and explicit undeclared/family-fallback telemetry.
contribution: A reusable event-to-declaration mapping engine with registry validation, dotted-path field extraction, loud undeclared handling, and gap-reporting hooks; it contributes a single source of render semantics that consumers can rely on without per-event parsing logic.
summary_for_embedding: This file implements a cached, validated registry lookup that converts raw session events into typed render declarations with field extraction, loud undeclared handling, and drop telemetry.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to let any UI or consumer render session outputs from a central, typed, fail-loud declaration registry instead of bespoke event parsers.

symbols.does: Exception raised when the registry JSON fails validation at load time.
symbols.description: RegistryInvalid signals that render_declarations.json did not pass closed-vocabulary, required-key, or field_map validation, preventing a half-valid registry from being served.

symbols.does: Registers a callable to receive undeclared and family-fallback drop records.
symbols.description: on_drop appends a hook function to _DROP_HOOKS if it is not already present, allowing external consumers such as the supervisor to observe registry gaps.

symbols.does: Calls each registered drop hook with a record, swallowing exceptions.
symbols.description: _fire_drop iterates over the hook list and invokes every hook with the drop record; any hook failure is silently ignored so that declaration processing continues uninterrupted.

symbols.does: Loads, validates, and caches the render declarations registry.
symbols.description: load_registry reads the adjacent JSON file, validates every declaration against closed vocabularies and schema rules, caches the result under a lock, and raises RegistryInvalid on any violation.

symbols.does: Resolves a dotted path against a nested dictionary root.
symbols.description: _resolve walks the path parts through dicts, returns the root for '.', and returns None for any missing segment so callers can treat absence as honest-absent.

symbols.does: Extracts declared fields from an event using a field_map.
symbols.description: _extract applies each dotted path in the field_map, omits missing values, and caps long strings at FIELD_CAP while adding a visible __truncated marker.

symbols.does: Returns the first matching declaration from an ordered candidate list.
symbols.description: _lookup scans candidate keys in priority order and returns the matched key, declaration, and a boolean indicating whether the match was a family wildcard ending in *.

symbols.does: Builds the ordered lookup candidates for a raw event.
symbols.description: derive_key produces most-specific-first candidates, with special handling for stream_event inner types, content block start/delta types, attachment types, and plain type/subtype events.

symbols.does: Builds lookup candidates for a content block.
symbols.description: _block_key prefers tool-name-specific keys for tool_use blocks, then block-type keys, then a content wildcard, enabling fine-grained declarations for individual tools.

symbols.does: Maps a single content block to its render declaration.
symbols.description: _declare_block resolves the block against the registry, returns UnknownEvent with a drop if nothing matches, and marks family_fallback when only a wildcard declaration matched.

symbols.does: Main public function that converts one raw event into a render declaration.
symbols.description: declare performs the top-level lookup, extracts fields, optionally dispatches nested content blocks via blocks_from, applies family_fallback and undeclared handling, and fires any resulting drop records.

symbols.does: Returns a sorted list of every render_key in the loaded registry.
symbols.description: declared_keys exposes the catalogue of registered declarations, useful for validation, discovery, and testing the completeness of the registry.
